lever_summary: Dense F64/I64 scatter. eval_scatter materialized operand.elements.to_vec() (a dense operand becomes a 24-byte Vec<Literal>) then copied/added Literal slices. New eval_scatter_dense scatters over a clone of the contiguous as_f64_slice()/as_i64_slice() operand, reading update slices from the typed updates backing — overwrite via copy_from_slice, add via a+b (F64) / a.wrapping_add(b) (I64) — and returns a dense output, bypassing all Literal machinery.

alien_graveyard.primitive: dense SoA / typed columnar storage applied to scatter
alien_graveyard.mapping: scatter contiguous native-typed slices instead of materializing + copying/adding 24-byte tagged-union elements

method: Same-worker comparison on ts2: dense (new_f64_values) operand + updates vs Vec<Literal>-backed operand + updates run in the same process; shared i64 indices.
command: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- scatter_256x256_f64 --save-baseline scat

change.lever: Added eval_scatter_dense(operand, updates, index_vals, slice_elems, dim0, index_mode, add_mode); eval_scatter calls it after the empty-update early return, returning None (generic path) unless both operand and updates are the same F64/I64 dense storage.

isomorphism.ordering: unchanged: same resolved index iteration order, same per-index base/update slice ranges; repeated indices apply in the same order (last-write-wins for overwrite, accumulate for add)
isomorphism.combiner: unchanged: overwrite via copy_from_slice; add via a+b (F64) / a.wrapping_add(b) (I64), matching binary_literal_op(Add) for those dtypes
isomorphism.index_modes: fill_or_drop (drops OOB) and clip (clamps) both validated; resolved by the shared resolve_axis0_index before the dense branch
isomorphism.errors: same checked_mul/checked_add overflow checks and the same slice-exceeds bounds checks (against the typed slice lengths == element counts)

validation.name: dense_scatter_parity
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax --lib --release scatter
validation.result: passed: 16 tests incl dense_scatter_matches_literal_path (overwrite + add x fill_or_drop + clip, OOB + repeated indices, f64 by bits + i64)

validation.name: clippy_deny_warnings
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-lax --release --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings
validation.result: passed (release dodges shared-target proc-macro rmeta drift in debug)
